title: Reform accused of proposing tax cuts worth £40bn to boost chances in Makerfield byelection – UK politics live
summary_full: Nigel Farage’s party has announced three uncosted proposals since the byelection campaign started, according to Conservative rivalsStarmer acknowledges some teenagers will get round these restrictons. But that does not make the rules pointless, he says.Will it mean that no child ever looks at social media again? No.But look, this might shock you, but it doesn’t shock parents of teenagers; they get around other laws too.Some technology companies want us to think that social media is unchangeable, part of an almost natural order.But we have to resist that kind of learned helplessness. We have agency, we can change it, and we will. Continue reading...
